thesis: Keweenaw Land Association owns more than 1.3 million mineral acres in Michigan and Wisconsin and collects lease income from Highland Copper's Copperwood project plus broader acreage options. **[Fact]** FY2024 mineral lease income was **$349,700** on roughly **1,126,284** shares (~**$12.70** GAAP book per share). **[Fact]** The company is not an operator; production royalties are off balance sheet until mining begins. Proof-first component math sums to about **$35.75** per share of economic value versus a price near **$55**, so the market embeds Copperwood and acreage optionality beyond filing-locked lease cash.
why_market_wrong: OTC illiquidity and GAAP historical-cost minerals hide the Copperwood royalty option; conversely, a move from management's **$27** repurchase to much higher quotes may over-discount delay risk. **[Inference]** At **$55**, roughly 4.3× book, dormant production royalties are partially priced in despite lease-only Lawrence base paths.

key_assumptions.source: [Assumption] SSI bridge scaled to spot copper

open_questions: Verify management cash-flow-positive-from-2026 claim against FY2025/H1 2026 filings.
Track Highland Copper Copperwood construction milestones and Michigan grant status.
Refresh spot copper and lease step-ups in mechanical market-input pass.
